cc: Robin Faisant, Esq.

Richard L, ltoore
Novenber t9 | L99L
The Ilonorable John A. Fisher
74 Alejandra Avenue
Atherton, California 
94027
Dear John:
This letter responds to yours of octohe]. 4. 1991. concerning
the possible application of the California Political Refom Act
(nActr) to the action of the Atherton civic Interest League
('rLeague'r) in sending its September 24, 1991, letter to all Tolrn
residents inforroing the recipients of the League's opposition to
proposed ordinance 456.
I have checked with our City Attorney on this natter. 
He
advises me that the Tolrn has no enforcement authority concerning
any possible violation of the Act in the nanner described. He has
said that if there is concern about a possible violation of the
Act, the proper investigating authorities would be either the San
Mateo county District Attorneyrs office in Redwood City, or the
Fair Political Practices Conmission in Sacranento.
Please let me know if you need further information.
Sincerely yours,
A,"E//r.e e/-7
christopher E. cobey
Mayor


JOHN A FISHER
74 ALEIANDRAAVENUE
ATTIERTON CA9WZ7
October 4, 1991
Hon. Christopher Cobey, Esq.
Town of Atherton
91 Ashfield Rd.
Arherton, CA 9402'7
RE: Atherton Civic Interest League Lobblng Effort:
Filing Requirement Under State of Califomia t-aw
Dear Chris:
I was recently chagrined to receive a September 24, l99l letter from the Atherton civic
Interest League recommending public opposition to ordinance #466 of the Town of
Atherton. This letter was addressed to all Atherton residents. I am sure you received a
copy.
This is a first in my experience with the Atherton civic Interest League. It appears to
constitute a lobblng activity directly affecting an ordinance of the Town. As a coniequence,
I am compelled to raise the question of whether the Civic Interest League should b" , t'iting
committee under the campaign Disclosure provisions of the political Reform Act and
whether they may be compelled to file California Form 420 and otherwise to establish
themselves as a lobblng organization.
I w-o-uld recommend that you obtain advice of rown counsel and of appropriate Sacramento
staff in order to advise the Civic Interest League.
I certainly regret their involvement in an active lobblng campaign. It seems inconsistent
with the historical charter and scope of the civic InterestLagui in the Town, but under the
circumstanceg I think the question of their status under the Fohticat Reform Act should be
properly addressed.
ohn A Fisher
Robin Faisant, Esq.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Atherton Civic Interest League's September 24, 1991 letter to town residents opposing a proposed ordinance constitutes lobbying activity requiring registration and filing under California's Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is incoming correspondence to a local official, not an FPPC advice letter. The document includes both an initial citizen request (Fisher to Mayor Cobey) and a non-FPPC response (Mayor Cobey's letter) referring the matter to FPPC. No formal FPPC response is present. The Mayor's letter indicates this should be directed to FPPC in Sacramento for proper investigation.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether the Atherton Civic Interest League's September 24, 1991 letter to town residents opposing a proposed ordinance constitutes lobbying activity requiring registration and filing under California's Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: This document contains a citizen's October 4, 1991 letter to the Town Mayor asking whether a civic organization's lobbying effort opposing a local ordinance triggers campaign disclosure and lobbying registration requirements, followed by the Mayor's November 19, 1991 response redirecting the inquiry to the San Mateo County District Attorney or FPPC as the proper investigating authorities.
